secondary_meaning: the sport played with a spherical ball on grass
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Known as 'soccer' in North America. Plural: footballs
common_collocations: jouer au football, match de football, équipe de football
image_context: A football/soccer match on a green grass field with players
example_1: Je joue au football avec mes amis le week-end.
example_1_translation: I play football with my friends on the weekend.
example_2: L'équipe de France a gagné le match de football.
example_2_translation: The French team won the football match.
example_3: Le football est le sport le plus populaire en France.
example_3_translation: Football is the most popular sport in France.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced foot-BOHL. Stress on second syllable. English loanword.
memory_hook: Football = foot (pied) + ball (ballon). Played with feet, not hands.
common_mistakes: In French, football = soccer. American football = football américain.

secondary_meaning: the sport played with a ball thrown into elevated baskets
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. English loanword. Plural: basketballs
common_collocations: jouer au basketball, match de basketball, panier de basketball
image_context: A basketball player shooting a ball into a hoop on a court
example_1: Mon frère adore jouer au basketball.
example_2: Le basketball est un sport populaire en Amérique.
example_2_translation: Basketball is a popular sport in America.
example_3: Nous regardons un match de basketball à la télévision.
example_3_translation: We are watching a basketball game on television.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced bask-et-BOHL. English loanword. Often shortened to 'basket'.
memory_hook: Basketball = basket (panier) + ball (ballon). Throw into the basket.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le basketball' (masculine). Often shortened to 'le basket'.

secondary_meaning: the act or sport of swimming in water
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Usually uncountable. Plural: natations (rare)
common_collocations: faire de la natation, championnat de natation, leçon de natation
image_context: A person swimming freestyle in a clear blue pool
example_1: Elle fait de la natation tous les matins.
example_2: Les leçons de natation sont dans la piscine municipale.
example_2_translation: The swimming lessons are at the municipal pool.
example_3: La natation est excellente pour la santé.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced nah-tah-see-OHN. Nasal 'on'. Stress on last syllable.
memory_hook: Natation = the activity of swimming. From Latin 'natare' (to swim).

secondary_meaning: a person trained to extinguish fires and rescue
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Feminine: 'la pompière' (rare). Plural: pompiers
common_collocations: pompier professionnel, camion de pompiers, chef pompier
image_context: A firefighter in uniform standing next to a red fire truck
example_1: Mon père est pompier depuis dix ans.
example_1_translation: My father has been a firefighter for ten years.
example_3_translation: She dreams of becoming a firefighter.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced pom-PYE. Stress on second syllable. From 'pompe' (pump).
memory_hook: Pompier = one who uses 'pompes' (pumps). Pumps water on fires.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le pompier' (masculine). Feminine form 'pompière' is modern and less common.

secondary_meaning: a person who serves food and drinks in a restaurant
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Feminine: 'serveuse'. Plural: serveurs
common_collocations: serveur au restaurant, appeler le serveur, bon serveur
image_context: A waiter in a restaurant holding a tray with glasses
example_1: Le serveur nous a apporté les menus.
example_1_translation: The waiter brought us the menus.
example_2: Elle travaille comme serveuse dans un restaurant.
example_2_translation: She works as a waitress in a restaurant.
example_3: Le serveur était très courtois et attentionné.
example_3_translation: The waiter was very polite and attentive.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced ser-VER. Stress on second syllable. From 'servir' (to serve).
memory_hook: Serveur = one who 'sert' (serves). Server in restaurant context.
common_mistakes: Feminine: 'serveuse' (not 'serveur' for women in modern usage).

secondary_meaning: a person trained to care for the sick or injured
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'l'' (before vowel). Masculine: 'infirmier'. Plural: infirmières
common_collocations: infirmière à l'hôpital, aide-infirmière, infirmière scolaire
image_context: A nurse in a hospital uniform with a clipboard
example_1_translation: The nurse gave me the medicine.
example_2: Mon rêve est de devenir infirmière.
example_2_translation: My dream is to become a nurse.
example_3: Les infirmières travaillent de longues heures.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced aw-fer-myeh. Stress on second syllable. From Latin 'infirmus' (weak).
memory_hook: Infirmière = from 'infirme' (infirm/sick). Cares for the infirm.

secondary_meaning: a small furry mammal kept as a pet
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Feminine: 'la chatte' (female cat). Plural: chats
common_collocations: chat noir, chat blanc, avoir un chat
image_context: A fluffy orange cat sitting and looking at the camera
example_1: Notre chat aime dormir sur le canapé.
example_1_translation: Our cat likes to sleep on the couch.
example_2: Le chat noir a traversé la rue.
example_2_translation: The black cat crossed the street.
example_3: Elle a un chat roux et très affectueux.
example_3_translation: She has a ginger cat that is very affectionate.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced shah. Short 'a' sound. From Old French.
memory_hook: Chat = the common house pet. French word for cat is very simple.
common_mistakes: Feminine: 'la chatte' (female). Don't confuse with 'cha' or 'chatte'.

secondary_meaning: a large hoofed animal used for riding or farming
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Plural is irregular: 'chevaux' (not 'chevales')
common_collocations: monter à cheval, course de chevaux, cheval blanc
image_context: A brown horse standing in a green meadow outdoors
example_1: Les enfants adorent faire du cheval.
example_2: Le cheval court dans le pré.
example_2_translation: The horse runs in the meadow.
example_3: Il y a une écurie avec dix chevaux.
example_3_translation: There is a stable with ten horses.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced shuh-VAHL. Stress on second syllable. 'ch' sounds like 'sh'.
memory_hook: Cheval = horse. From Latin 'caballus'. Equine animal.

secondary_meaning: a small furry mammal with long ears
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Feminine: 'la lapine'. Plural: lapins
common_collocations: lapin blanc, lapin de compagnie, manger des carottes
image_context: A cute white rabbit with long ears in a garden
example_1: Mon fils a un lapin comme animal de compagnie.
example_1_translation: My son has a rabbit as a pet.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced lah-PAHN. Stress on second syllable. Nasal 'in' sound.
memory_hook: Lapin = rabbit. From Old French 'lepretin'. Associated with carrots.

secondary_meaning: a large area of land covered with trees
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Common in nature contexts. Plural: forêts
common_collocations: grande forêt, forêt vierge, marcher dans la forêt
image_context: A dense green forest with tall trees and sunlight filtering through
example_1: Nous avons fait une promenade dans la forêt.
example_1_translation: We took a walk in the forest.
example_2: La forêt est magnifique en automne.
example_2_translation: The forest is beautiful in autumn.
example_3: Beaucoup d'animaux vivent dans la forêt.
example_3_translation: Many animals live in the forest.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced foh-RET. Stress on second syllable. From Latin 'forestis'.
memory_hook: Forêt = forest. A place of many trees and nature.

secondary_meaning: a very high hill of rock and earth
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Very common in French geography. Plural: montagnes
common_collocations: haute montagne, chaîne de montagnes, sommets montagne
image_context: A majestic mountain peak with snow cap and green slopes
example_1: En été, nous allons à la montagne.
example_1_translation: In summer, we go to the mountain.
example_2: La montagne est couverte de neige en hiver.
example_2_translation: The mountain is covered with snow in winter.
example_3: Elle aime faire de la randonnée en montagne.
example_3_translation: She likes hiking in the mountains.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced mon-TAH-nyuh. Stress on second syllable. Soft 'gn' sound.
memory_hook: Montagne = mount + -agne. A high geological formation.

secondary_meaning: a large natural stream of water flowing to the sea
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Note: B1 level. Plural: rivières
common_collocations: rivière profonde, courant de rivière, traverser une rivière
image_context: A peaceful river flowing through a green landscape with rocks
example_1: Les enfants jouent près de la rivière.
example_1_translation: The children play near the river.
example_2: On peut faire du kayak sur cette rivière.
example_2_translation: We can go kayaking on this river.
example_3: La rivière se jette dans le fleuve.
example_3_translation: The river flows into the larger river.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced ree-vee-YEH. Stress on last syllable. Guttural 'r'.
memory_hook: Rivière = from Latin 'riparia' (riverbank). River flowing to sea.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la rivière' (feminine). B1 level vocabulary (more advanced).

secondary_meaning: the annual Christian festival celebrating the birth of Jesus
usage_notes: Proper noun - no article. Pronounced with nasal 'o'. Not pluralized normally.
common_collocations: Joyeux Noël, Noël dernier, avant Noël, sapin de Noël
image_context: A beautifully decorated Christmas tree with lights and presents underneath
example_1: Nous décorons la maison pour Noël.
example_1_translation: We decorate the house for Christmas.
example_2_translation: Merry Christmas and Happy New Year!
example_3: Les enfants reçoivent des cadeaux à Noël.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced noh-EHL. Nasal 'o' sound. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: Noël = from Latin 'Natale' (birth). Jesus's birth celebration.
common_mistakes: Proper noun - no article. Capitalized. Not 'Noel' (English spelling).

usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Opposite of 'gauche' (left). Plural: droites
common_collocations: à droite, tourner à droite, sur votre droite
image_context: A road sign pointing right with an arrow indicating direction
example_1: Tournez à droite au coin de la rue.
example_1_translation: Turn right at the corner of the street.
example_2: La boulangerie est sur votre droite.
example_2_translation: The bakery is on your right.
example_3: Va à droite, pas à gauche.
example_3_translation: Go to the right, not to the left.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced dah-DWAH. Stress on second syllable. From Latin 'directa'.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la droite' (feminine). Not to be confused with 'droit' (right/law).

secondary_meaning: organized sounds and silences creating an artistic form
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Usually uncountable. Plural: musiques (rare, for styles)
common_collocations: musique classique, écouter de la musique, professeur de musique
image_context: Sheet music with musical notes and a piano in the background
example_1: J'écoute de la musique pour me détendre.
example_1_translation: I listen to music to relax.
example_2: Elle joue de la musique classique au piano.
example_2_translation: She plays classical music on the piano.
example_3: La musique est l'un de mes hobbies.
example_3_translation: Music is one of my hobbies.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced myu-ZEEK. Stress on second syllable. From Greek 'mousiké'.
memory_hook: Musique = music. From the Muses in Greek mythology.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la musique' (feminine). 'Musique' also means 'sound' in some contexts.

secondary_meaning: a motion picture; a cinema production
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. English loanword. Plural: films
common_collocations: film d'action, voir un film, film comique
image_context: A movie theater screen showing an exciting action scene
example_1: Nous avons regardé un film au cinéma.
example_1_translation: We watched a movie at the cinema.
example_3: Ce film est très intéressant et captivant.
example_3_translation: This film is very interesting and captivating.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced FEELM. One syllable. English loanword adopted into French.
memory_hook: Film = movie. English word used directly in French.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le film' (masculine). Common English loanword in French.
